channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alex Jones keeps Infowars for now after judge rejects The Onion's winning auction bid
item.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected the auction sale of Alex Jones’ Infowars to The Onion satirical news outlet, criticizing the bidding for the conspiracy theory platform as flawed as well as how much money families of the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary shooting stood to receive.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The in-school offerings are in addition to the After School Satan Clubs, which launched in 2020 and have risen in popularity among parents seeking alternatives to religious clubs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KREX 5

item.title: California man found 25 years after going missing discovered to be registered sex offender
item.description: A California man who had been missing for 25 years was identified by his sister when she saw his photo in a news article, but the discovery unveiled disturbing details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps FTC Commissioner Andrew Ferguson to chair agency
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Andrew Ferguson, a commissioner at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), as the next head of the independent agency.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled to Eastern Europe is in custody, online records show
item.description: Online records show a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left behind a wife and three children to go to Eastern Europe is in a Wisconsin jail.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FBI says 2 Homeland Security agents in Utah sold illegal drugs for profit through informant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Two Department of Homeland Security agents in Utah sold drugs that were previously seized as evidence through an informant in an illegal scheme that brought in up to $300,000, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Monarch butterflies to be listed as a threatened species in US
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — U.S. wildlife officials announced a decision Tuesday to extend federal protections to monarch butterflies after years of warnings from environmentalists that populations are shrinking and the beloved pollinator may not survive climate change. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Belichick's known ties to the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
item.description: Belichick, 72, is considered by many to be the greatest NFL head coach of all time and was the architect of the New England Patriots dynasty from 2001-2019.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Geminids meteor shower peaks this weekend: How to watch
item.description: People across the globe could see up to 120 meteors per hour during the peak of the Geminids meteor shower this week.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Altoona McDonald's slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: An Altoona McDonald's is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the murder of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pepperdine students shelter in library, campus center as Malibu fire forces evacuations
item.description: A shelter-in-place order for Pepperdine University is in effect until daylight Tuesday as a result of the Franklin Fire in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami's Ward, Oregon's Gabriel, Colorado's Hunter, Boise State’s Jeanty, are named Heisman finalists
item.description: Oregon's Dillon Gabriel, Colorado's Travis Hunter, Boise State’s Ashton Jeanty, and Miami's Cam Ward were announced as the Heisman Trophy finalists on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON AP College Football Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Poet and literary celebrity Nikki Giovanni dies at 81
item.description: The poet, author, educator and public speaker who rose from borrowing money to release her first book to decades as a literary celebrity sharing her blunt and conversational takes on everything from racism and love to space travel and mortality, has died. She was 81.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Descendant of last native leader of Alaska island demands Japanese reparations for 1942 invasion
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Helena Pagano's great-grandfather was the last Alaska Native chief of a remote island in the Bering Sea, closer to Russia than North America.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Luigi Mangione speaks out following arrest and arraignment
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Mother arrested after posting video of herself kicking infant: police
item.description: A woman accused of kicking an infant multiple times has been arrested after she uploaded video of the abuse online, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oldest known stone tablet inscribed with Ten Commandments to be auctioned
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments, dating from 300 to 800 A.D., will be sold in New York this month, Sotheby's auction house said. The 155-pound (52-kilogram) marble slab inscribed with the commandments in Paleo-Hebrew script will be auctioned on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny found not guilty in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Duck rescued from lake after getting tangled in fishing line, lure
item.description: Colorado deputies are asking people to correctly dispose of fishing lines and hooks after authorities waded through cold waters to rescue a tangled young bird.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Darnold delivers for Vikings with career-high 347 yards and 5 TDs to beat Falcons, Cousins 42-21
item.description: Darnold added yet another highlight to his brilliant first season with the Vikings, passing for 347 yards and five touchdowns, both career highs, in a 42-21 victory over the foundering Falcons on Sunday to stretch their winning streak to six games.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hemingway look-alikes visit Cuba and some of the late writer's favorite places
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Ernest Hemingway has returned to Cuba in a way. Eighteen white-bearded men who resemble the late U.S. author arrived in Havana to visit some of his favorite places when he lived on the island decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First 12-team College Football Playoff set, Oregon seeded No. 1 and SMU edges Alabama for last spot
item.description: The tournament starts Dec. 20-21 with four first-round games involving teams seeded 5-12. It concludes Jan. 20 with the national title game in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These states have the highest — and lowest — median salary: new data
item.description: New data released by a payroll site found median annual salaries from $40,000 to over six figures across the country.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial urged jurors on Friday to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn't reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4
